description:
About (CHA), Coordination of Humanitarian Assistance موسسه همکاری
Coordination of Humanitarian Assistance (CHA) is a non-governmental, non-profitable, and non-political Afghan NGO, working since 1987 mainly in agriculture, health, education, and infrastructure sectors in more vulnerable areas of the country. CHA is working through the implementation of Emergency, rehabilitation, and development projects for emergency aid for war, famine, and natural disaster-affected families in the field, to assist with the rehabilitation of rural and urban life, and to work with communities for sustainable development in Afghanistan.
Core Commitments and Expectations from Candidates:
CHA is dedicated to creating an environment where all staff members are committed to our core values and principles. We believe that our employees play a crucial role in maintaining the integrity and excellence of our organization. Therefore, we have outlined the following core commitments and expectations that all candidates must adhere to:
Adherence to CHA's Policies and Procedures: All candidates must adhere to CHA's policies and procedures, these guidelines are designed to ensure a safe, fair, and efficient working environment. Compliance with these policies is fundamental to maintaining the standards and quality of our operation
Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA): CHA has zero tolerance for sexual exploitation, sexual harassment and abuse. All candidates, employees, associates, and partners must comply with the PSEA policy.
Ethical Conduct and Integrity: Candidates are expected to maintain high standards of ethical conduct and integrity in all their professional dealings. This includes avoiding conflicts of interest, maintaining confidentiality, and adhering to anti-fraud and anti-corruption guidelines​​.
Commitment to Non-Discrimination and Diversity: CHA is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment. Candidates must respect and promote diversity and non-discrimination based on race, gender, age, or any other protected characteristic​​.
Child Protection: Candidates must adhere to the Child Protection Policy, ensuring the safety and well-being of children in all CHA activities.
Environmental and Social Safeguards: Candidate must follow CHA's Environmental and Social Safeguard Policy to minimize environmental impact and ensure sustainable practices in all projects and operations​​.
Confidentiality and Data Protection: Candidates are required to maintain the confidentiality of all CHA data and information.
All selected candidates will be expected to adhere to these standards and principles. To ensure compliance, candidates will undergo rigorous reference and background checks. These checks will include the verification of academic credentials and employment history. Selected candidates may also be required to provide additional information to facilitate these checks.
By joining CHA, candidates commit to these core values and contribute to our mission of providing humanitarian assistance with the highest level of integrity and professionalism. We look forward to welcoming dedicated individuals who share our commitment to making a positive impact.

Job Description
• Develop and regularly update work plans and implementation schedules for all livelihood-related projects.
• Lead and oversee the execution of MSME development, carpet weaving, value chain strengthening, teacher training, computer learning, and cash-for-work initiatives.
• Provide technical expertise, guidance, and mentoring to project teams, trainers, and implementing partners.
• Coordinate closely with provincial teams, government departments, local authorities, and community leaders, while maintaining strong collaboration with UNHCR focal points on planning, reporting, and monitoring.
• Monitor project activities to ensure quality, effectiveness, sustainability, and timely delivery.
• Collect, analyze, and document data, success stories, and beneficiary feedback for inclusion in reports.
• Prepare and submit high-quality progress reports (weekly, monthly, and quarterly) in line with project manager and donor (UNHCR) requirements.
• Strengthen the capacity of field staff through technical support and ongoing mentoring.
• Conduct other relevant tasks and duties

Job Requirements
• Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture, and business administration(Master’s degree strongly preferred).
• Minimum of 5 years of proven experience in implementing livelihood programs, particularly MSME and agriculture-focused initiatives.
• Preference will be given to candidates from the relevant provinces.
• Demonstrated experience working with returnees, IDPs, and vulnerable groups in complex and challenging contexts.
• Strong technical knowledge of MSME development, carpet weaving, value chain strengthening, teacher training, computer literacy, and cash-for-work programming.
• Prior experience with UNHCR-funded projects or other international donor-funded programs is highly desirable.
• Proficiency in English, Dari, and Pashto.
• Excellent communication, coordination, and report-writing skills.
• Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.
• Willingness and ability to travel regularly to field sites across the northern provinces.

title: IT Officer NOC Engineer - Night Shift
description: Role Summary To serve as the frontline guardian of Setaragan Mutahed’s high-volume Airtime Top-Up infrastructure and StarPay EMI. This role ensures 24/7 availability of critical financial transactions and telecommunication links. You will monitor production systems, mitigate real-time payment failures, and ensure that overnight batch settlements and EMI reconciliations are processed without error, providing a seamless experience for thousands of agents and end-users. Duties and Responsibilities • Continuous Infrastructure & Transaction Monitoring: Proactively monitor core infrastructure, server health, and network links. Use approved tools to track the real-time flow of Top-Up transactions and StarPay EMI payments, identifying any latency or drops in success rates. • L1 Incident Triage & FinTech Troubleshooting: Perform immediate first-level triage on system alarms. For payment failures or "timeout" errors, collect critical evidence (API logs, transaction IDs, screenshots) and execute approved runbooks to restore service. • Critical Escalation Management: Act as the primary point of contact during the night shift. Escalate high-severity incidents (e.g., core database downtime, ISP link failure, or payment gateway outages) to on-call Tier-2 engineers or vendor support (MNOs/Banks) according to the strict Escalation Matrix. • Rigorous Ticketing & Shift Handover: Document every incident in the Jira/Ticketing system with high precision. Provide a comprehensive Morning Handover Report detailing pending issues, vendor tickets, and critical status updates to the daytime operations team. • Operational Health Checks: Conduct routine checks on server CPU/RAM, disk usage, and service statuses. Ensure all scheduled automated jobs and security backups are completed, reporting any exceptions immediately to prevent morning backlog. • Overnight Batch & EMI Reconciliation: Verify the successful execution of overnight batch reconciliation jobs between StarPay and partner banks. Manually trigger or report failures in settlement processes to ensure financial data integrity for the next business day. • Network & ISP Coordination: Support basic network troubleshooting (link down, packet loss) affecting agent connectivity. Coordinate directly with local ISPs and MNO partners (AWCC, Roshan, Etisalat, MTN, Salaam) to resolve connectivity issues affecting the Top-Up platform. • Compliance & Security Adherence: Maintain absolute confidentiality of sensitive financial and customer data. Follow strict Access Control Procedures and Zero-Change Policy—do not perform unauthorized system changes or configuration updates during the shift. Job Requirements • Education: Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Computer Science, Information Technology, or Software Engineering. • Experience: 1–3 years of experience in a Network Operations Center (NOC), IT Support, or FinTech operations role. Fresh graduates with exceptional understanding of Linux/Networking and a high sense of responsibility will be considered. • Shift Flexibility: Absolute willingness to work permanent night shifts and remain alert during quiet hours to handle sudden, time-critical emergencies. Technical Skills • Networking Foundations: Strong grasp of TCP/IP, DNS, VPNs, and troubleshooting tools (Ping, MTR, Traceroute, Telnet). • Systems Knowledge: Familiarity with Windows/Linux environments, basic SQL queries (for transaction lookups), and monitoring dashboards (e.g., Zabbix, Grafana, or PRTG). • FinTech Literacy: Understanding of API response codes (200, 404, 500) and the basic flow of a digital payment transaction. • Documentation: High proficiency in professional English/Local language for writing technical incident logs and handover notes. Soft Skills & Behavioral Competencies • Crisis Management: Ability to remain calm and methodical when multiple systems trigger alarms simultaneously. • Sense of Ownership: A "pro-active" rather than "reactive" mindset; taking responsibility for a ticket until it is resolved or officially handed over. • Reliability: Strict punctuality and adherence to operational procedures, recognizing that StarPay’s reputation relies on 100% system uptime. • Communication: Clear, concise communication skills to explain technical issues to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders. Submission Guidelines Interested and qualified national candidates are invited to click the link below and fill in the form completely. submit their applications, including a cover letter and an updated CV , no later than 05 May, 2026 to the link provided below: Admission Link: ( https://forms.gle/jANFWERiLvYSTRwc7 ) Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: Specialist IT Security
description: Role Summary Specialist IT Security with Etisalat Afghanistan. Duties and Responsibilities • Monitor and analyze network traffic, system activities, and security events to detect potential intrusions, attacks, or vulnerabilities. • Respond to, investigate, and remediate security incidents, ensuring timely resolution and proper escalation where required. • Perform regular risk assessments, vulnerability assessments, and security audits to identify and mitigate potential risks. • Conduct penetration testing and security validation exercises to proactively identify weaknesses in systems, applications, and network infrastructure. • Prepare detailed incident reports, root cause analyses, and recommendations for continuous improvement. • Manage, maintain, and troubleshoot security infrastructure, including firewalls, VPN (SSL/IPsec), encryption systems, DMZ environments, and endpoint protection solutions. • Monitor endpoints using EDR and NDR solutions to detect, analyze, and respond to advanced threats. • Utilize SIEM & SOAR platforms for log monitoring, correlation, and threat detection. • Monitor and secure Active Directory and identity systems using relevant security tools. • Administer and support security solutions such as NAC, PAM, and IAM, including daily operations and troubleshooting. • Integrate network devices and servers (Linux/Windows) with SIEM and other centralized security platforms. • Collaborate with internal teams and attend technical sessions to ensure alignment of security operations and initiatives. • Support the implementation and follow-up of cybersecurity projects and initiatives across the organization. • Promote security awareness by guiding and training staff on cybersecurity best practices. • Stay updated with the latest cybersecurity threats, vulnerabilities, and technologies to enhance the organization’s security posture. • Perform any additional tasks assigned by management in line with organizational objectives. Job Requirements Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or a related field, with at least four years of relevant experience preferred. title: MSupply/Data entry assistant
description:
About MHCO
Maihan Humanitarian Convergence Organization (MHCO) is a national non-government, non-political, and non-profit organization registered with the Ministry of Economy, committed to delivering humanitarian services across Afghanistan. MHCO focuses on various sectors including Health, Education, Protection, Nutrition, Livelihoods, Food Security, and Organizational Development.

Job Description
he MSupply/Data Entry Assistant supports the pharmacy department by ensuring accurate and timely entry of stock data into the mSupply system, maintaining proper inventory records, and assisting with reporting, stock monitoring, and documentation. The role contributes to efficient pharmaceutical supply management and ensures compliance with organizational procedures.
Duties and Responsibilities:
Accurately enter and update pharmaceutical data in the mSupply system , including stock receipts, issues, adjustments, and transfers.
Maintain up-to-date records of medicines and medical supplies in both physical and electronic systems.
Verify and cross-check data with stock cards, bin cards, and delivery notes to ensure accuracy.
Monitor stock levels and report low stock, expiries, and discrepancies to the supervisor.
Assist in preparing stock reports, consumption reports, and inventory summaries on a regular basis.
Ensure proper documentation and filing of all pharmacy records, including invoices, waybills, and stock reports.
Support the pharmacy team during stock counts, physical inventory, and audits.
Maintain confidentiality and integrity of all data entered into the system.
Coordinate with warehouse and pharmacy staff to ensure timely data entry of all transactions.
Identify and report any inconsistencies or errors in the system promptly.
Assist in ensuring compliance with organizational policies and donor requirements related to pharmacy and inventory management.
Perform any other related tasks assigned by the supervisor.

Job Requirements
Bachelor’s degree in Pharmacy, Health Sciences, Computer Science , Business Administration, or a related field.
At least 2 –3 years of relevant experience in data entry, pharmacy, or supply chain/warehouse management.
Familiarity with mSupply system or other inventory management software is preferred.
Good knowledge of pharmaceutical products and stock management practices.
Strong data entry skills with high attention to accuracy and detail.
Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word) .
Ability to maintain organized records and documentation.
Good communication and coordination skills.
Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
Understanding of basic reporting and documentation procedures.
Knowledge of English and local languages (Dari/Pashto) is required.
Strong sense of integrity, confidentiality, and accountability.

We do not tolerate any form of inappropriate behaviour, including sexual harassment, exploitation, abuse, or financial misconduct. All MHCO staff are expected to uphold these principles in accordance with our Code of Conduct and safeguarding policies. We place strong emphasis on recruiting individuals who demonstrate integrity and align with our organizational values.

title: Chief Information Officer
description: Role Summary The Chief Information Officer (CIO) is a senior executive responsible for leading the bank’s overall information technology (IT) strategy and operations. The CIO ensures that technology systems support the bank’s strategic goals, enhance operational efficiency, and comply with regulatory requirements. This role involves overseeing IT infrastructure, digital banking systems, cybersecurity frameworks, and data management practices. The CIO plays a critical role in driving digital transformation, improving customer experience through technology, and ensuring the security and integrity of all banking systems. The CIO collaborates with executive management and other departments to identify technological needs, implement innovative solutions, and manage IT risks. Additionally, the role ensures business continuity, system reliability, and alignment of IT initiatives with the bank’s long-term objectives. Duties and Responsibilities Chief Information Officer shall develop goals, roadmap and executing a multi-year technology strategy supporting the full range of business services, and ensure the IT department runs smoothly and effectively. Chief Information Officer shall provide vision and leadership for developing and implementing information technology initiatives and Chief Information Officer is a member of the Executive Leadership Team and directs the planning and implementation of enterprise IT systems in support of business operations in order to improve cost effectiveness and service quality, Chief Information Officer shall direct activities of the IT Department, providing leadership and supervising of IT staff and ensures Compliance with bank’s policies and procedures as well monitor changes in the technology sector to discover ways the company can improve and develop. Chief Information Officer is responsible for daily operations of technology and technology-related systems and in providing ongoing service to internal staff and other stakeholders, also responsible for all aspects of the organization’s information technology and systems, including information and cybersecurity. Chief Information Officer Shall Lead IT strategic and operational planning to achieve business goals by fostering innovation, prioritizing IT initiatives, and coordinating the evaluation, deployment and management of current and future IT resources and systems across the organization. Chief Information Officer Shall Create state of the organization dynamic security practices across the IT department and bank as well provide high quality day-to-day operations of technology and technology enabled business functions. Chief Information Officer shall develop and maintain an appropriate IT organizational structure that supports the organization’s needs, including information and cybersecurity. Chief Information Officer shall stay abreast of emerging technologies that can improve IT department performance and bring cost effectiveness to the company. Chief Information Officer responsible to stay at the forefront of IT risk and security issues facing the Bank also Assess and make recommendations on the improvement or re-engineering of the IT department. Chief Information Officer shall maintain security standards appropriate for the bank’s size and complexity and Develop, track, and control the IT annual operating and capital budgets. Chief Information Officer shall collaborate with senior leaders to ensure technology strategy and investment are in line with business function objectives and Acting as a senior point of escalation to troubleshoot, report and resolve issues as well supervise the networks and computer systems including Core Banking applications and Databases in the company to ensure optimal performance. Chief Information Officer shall serve as the senior technical solutions analyst and ensuring strategic technology decisions are evaluated and assessed in line with technology industry standards. Chief Information Officer shall identify security threats, formulate strategies and methods to mitigate the threats. Chief Information Officer Shall Plan, Direct and establish IT-related projects as well create and adapt technological platforms to improve the customer experience. Chief Information Officer is responsible for Troubleshoot date-related issues and establish regular maintenance. Chief Information Officer shall work with upper-level IT staff to develop and implement department goals and implementing IT policies, protocols and standards. Chief Information officer shall Develops and implement strategies to improve the efficiency and efficacy of technologies initiatives appropriate support to company-wide goals. Chief Information Officer is responsible to review performance of IT staff and develop their Job Descriptions. Chief Information Officer is Responsible for the development, review and certification of all back-up and disaster recovery procedures and plans as well planning data storage and monitoring performance of databases. Chief Information officer shall Train IT staff personnel to enhance their skills and knowledge. Chief Information officer shall monitor and regular check-up of IT succession plan and develop succession plan for key IT positions. Chief Information Officer shall work with upper management and senior IT staff to develop the IT department budget as well review and recommend IT department purchases and payments. Chief Information officer shall Review Vendor proposals and makes decisions based on things such as price, what they offer, and how the vendor product may fit into the organization. Chief Information officer shall communicate with other executives regarding costs, value and risk-potential of new IT projects. Chief Information Officer Shall Oversees the development and testing of new programs developed specifically for the company. Chief Information Officer Shall Ensures the company’s data remains secure by keeping up to date on the latest cyber security threats and finds ways to guard against them on an organization-wide scale. Chief Information officer shall Coaches members of the IT teams, particularly IT leaders, so everyone stays on the same pate across the company. Chief Information officer shall Communicates with other executive members to make sure all parts of the company use technology in the best ways possible. To attend to any other matter that may not have been mentioned in this document but is otherwise required to be attended by the job holder. Besides, the Job Holder is to attend to any Bank related assignment given by CEO, Board of Supervisors or General Meeting of Share Holders. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science required; Master’s degree preferred. Professional Certificates in cybersecurity, information Technology, and modern threats. Experience: Minimum 7-10 Year experience in managing or directing IT operation in financial institutions. Skills: Proven experience as chief information officer or similar managerial role. Excellent knowledge of IT systems and infrastructure. Background in designing/developing IT systems and planning implementation. Solid understanding of data analysis, budgeting and business operations. Superior analytical and problem-solving capabilities. Strong strategic and business mindset. Excellent organizational and leadership skills. Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities. title: Commercial Reporting Officer
description: Role Summary The reporting officer is responsible for ensuring the accurate collection, validation, analysis, and reporting of all commercial data across key businesses, including Top-Up, Star Pay, and Shops. The role supports strategic decision-making, performance monitoring, and continuous improvement through reliable MIS systems and actionable insights. Duties and Responsibilities 1. Data Collection & Management • Lead the systematic collection of data from all commercial projects, regional teams, and operational channels. • Ensure data integrity by maintaining structured, reliable, and well-organized databases. • Implement data validation checks to ensure accuracy, completeness, and consistency across all reporting sources. 2. Reporting & Business Analysis • Prepare and deliver high-quality daily, weekly, and monthly reports for the Head of Commercial and senior management. • Monitor and report KPIs, sales performance, and regional performance metrics. • Conduct detailed data analysis to identify trends, performance gaps, and growth opportunities. • Provide clear, actionable insights to support strategic and operational decision-making. 3. Performance Monitoring & KPI Tracking • Track and monitor targets, and operational KPIs across Top-Up, Star Pay, and Shops projects. • Ensure real-time visibility of performance against defined business objectives. • Highlight underperformance or risk areas and escalate to relevant stakeholders for timely corrective action. 4. Cross-Functional Coordination & Support • Collaborate closely with product owners, main office, and regional teams to ensure timely and accurate data flow. • Provide standardized reporting formats, dashboards, and templates to support operations. 5. Process Improvement & Automation • Continuously evaluate existing reporting processes and identify inefficiencies or gaps. • Recommend and implement improvements to enhance reporting accuracy, speed, and usability. • Drive automation initiatives to reduce manual reporting efforts and improve data reliability. • Support system enhancements and digital transformation of MIS processes where applicable. 6. Data Governance & Documentation • Maintain proper documentation of reporting structures, data sources, and KPI definitions. • Ensure consistency in reporting standards across all business units. • Support audit requirements by ensuring data traceability and historical record management. Job Requirements Education • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Statistics, Data Science, or a related field • A Master’s degree or relevant certifications in Data Analysis, MIS, or Business Intelligence is an advantage Experience • Minimum 2–4 years of experience in reporting, MIS, data analysis, or a similar role • Experience in telecom, fintech, banking, or FMCG sectors is preferred • Proven experience in handling large datasets, reporting systems, and performance tracking Skills & Competencies • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with the ability to interpret complex data • High attention to accuracy, detail, and data integrity • Ability to translate data into actionable insights for business decisions • Strong understanding of KPIs, performance tracking, and business metrics • Excellent problem-solving and process improvement skills • Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data analysis tools) • Experience with BI tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is highly preferred • Familiarity with databases, SQL, or data management systems is an advantage • Experience with ERP/CRM systems and reporting dashboards • Ability to design and maintain automated reports and dashboards Submission Guidelines Application Submission Guidelines Interested and qualified national candidates are invited to click the link below and fill in the form completely. submit their applications, including a cover letter and an updated CV , no later than 10 May, 2026 to the link provided below: Submission Form: ( https://forms.gle/WNnFXjsFKYVzEGdC6 ) Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: Payment Collection Officer (Female is preferable)
description:
About City Connect ICT Services
CityConnect is a Kabul-based internet service provider that has been delivering reliable connectivity solutions across Afghanistan for over 10 years. With a strong focus on quality service, customer satisfaction, and technological innovation, CityConnect has built a solid reputation in the market. The company is committed to expanding its network infrastructure and providing stable, high-speed internet services to businesses and individuals throughout the country.

Job Description
Payment Collection & Follow-ups:
Contact customers and visits to remind them of overdue payments.
Follow up on unpaid invoices and ensure timely collection of outstanding amounts.
Maintain proper records of collections and pending payments/ invoices.
Customer Interaction & Support:
Address customer inquiries regarding their bills, payment methods, and outstanding balances.
Negotiate payment plans with customers who face financial difficulties.
Provide receipts and ensure smooth transactions.
Billing & Account Management:
Verify customer account details and ensure billing accuracy.
Coordinate with the relevant authority to resolve any discrepancies.
Keep records of defaulters and escalate cases as needed.
Legal & Recovery Process:
Work with legal teams for the recovery of long-overdue payments.
Issue warnings and final notices to non-paying customers.
Recommend disconnection of services for defaulters.
Feedback & Coordination:
Work closely with the finance and sales teams to improve collection efficiency.
Provide feedback on customer behavior and potential risks.

Job Requirements
1. High school is required
2. Ability to handle difficult customers professionally.
3. Self-motivated with an ability to achieve 100% targets/ collections.
4. Must be quite enough familiar in Kabul City.
5. The candidate must be able to ride a motorbike in Kabul city for payment collections.
6. Strong negotiation and communication skills.
7. Attention to detail and problem-solving abilities.
8. Basic understanding of legal procedures related to debt collection
9. Fluency in local languages i.e. Pashto / Dari is required. knowledge of English language is an Asset.
10. at least one year of experience in payment collections or accounts receivable (preferably in an ISP or telecom company)

Submission Guideline
Applicants meeting the above requirements are requested to apply for this vacancy through email stated above only.
Important Notes:
1. Please attach your CV and Tazkira for further screening purpose.
2. Only short-listed candidates will be contacted for further assessment.

Submission Email
hr@cityconnect.af

details.Experience: Must have at least 2 years experience at Internet Service Provider Company
details.Education: 12 Grade or Bachelor degree is desirable.
details.Years of Experience: Must have at least 2 years experience at Internet Service Provider Company

title: Digital Sales Officer
description:
About Muslim English Language Institute
We and Our Inspiring Story!
About MELI
Thriving in the industry for twenty three years, our team consists of over five hundred employees who possess high professional expertise and strive to specialize in a single field; English language. Our vision is to enable a maximum number of people to speak the international language, the international way. With English being the lingua franca of today’s world, we take pride in specializing in teaching the language.
Today’s MELI
Across our 10 branches, we host tens of thousands of students! Within our 250 classrooms, MELI offers up to 240 new classes in a single week under the supervision of hundreds of English language specialists.

Job Description
JOB SUMMARY
The Digital Sales Officer plays a pivotal role in driving the growth of Muslim English Language Institute (MELI) through innovative digital marketing strategies. This position holder is responsible for developing and executing comprehensive digital sales plans, attracting online leads, and converting them into paying students.
RESPONSIBILITIES
• Lead Generation: Develop and implement effective digital marketing campaigns to attract potential students through various social media platforms, search engine optimization (SEO), and other online channels.
• Conversion Optimization: Create and optimize landing pages, sales funnels, and user experiences to maximize conversion rates and drive student enrollment.
• Customer Relationship Management (CRM): Utilize CRM tools to manage and nurture leads, track student progress, and provide exceptional customer service.
• Data Analysis: Analyze website traffic, conversion rates, and customer behavior to identify trends and optimize digital marketing strategies.
• Sales Team Coordination: Collaborate with the sales team to ensure a seamless transition of leads and provide necessary support for closing deals.
• Reporting and Analysis: Prepare regular reports on digital marketing performance, including key metrics, ROI, and recommendations for improvement.
• Stay Updated: Stay informed about the latest digital marketing trends, technologies, and best practices to maintain a competitive edge.
• Adherence to MELI policies and procedures to ensure a smooth and productive working experience.
• Undertake any other duties as assigned by the Marketing Manager.

Job Requirements
REQUIREMENTS
• Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Proven experience in digital marketing, with a strong focus on lead generation and conversion optimization.
• Proficiency in social media marketing platforms (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, etc.), SEO, and email marketing.
• Strong analytical skills and ability to interpret data to drive decision-making.
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
• Possess qualities of humility, maturity, respectfulness, honesty, and integrity.
• Excellent written and spoken English skills.
• Have command of both National Languages (Dari & Pashto)

Submission Guideline
For more details kindly open the link and complete the form to apply.
https://forms.gle/XtAr2pEe8o4VyYgP6
Please note that only shortlisted applicants will be contacted and called for a test/Interview.

Submission Email
https://forms.gle/XtAr2pEe8o4VyYgP6

title: آفیسر ارشد انجینر نرم افزار
description:
Role Summary
عنوان وظیفه : آفیسر ارشد انجینر نرم افزار
آمریت: آمریت عمومی تکنالوژی معلوماتی
گرید : هفتم (۷)
تعداد بست : یک (۱)
تاریخ آغاز: ۱۴۰۵/۰۲/۰۸
تاریخ ختم : ۱۴۰۵/۰۲/۲۲
نوع اعلان : داخلی و خارجی
نوع استخدام: دائمی

Duties and Responsibilities
· انجام در عملی کردن کامل لایف سایکل توسعه نرم افزار (SDLC)
· ایجاد اسناد در توسعه نودارها، طرح ها و مستندات برای شناسایی نیازمندی ها و راحل ها
· ایجاد کدهای که به خوبی طراحی شده، قابلیت تست داشته و مطابق با استندارد های کد نویسی باشد
· طراحی نمودار های پروسه توسعه، طرح های سیستم و مستندات لازم برای شناسایی نیاز ها و ارائه راه حل های مناسب
· یکپارچه سازی اجزای نرم افزار به یک سیستم نرم افزاری کاملاً کاربردی
· ادغام بخش های مختلف نرم افزار برای ایجاد یک سیستم کامل و کارا
· طراحی برنامه های آزمایش و روش های تضمین کیفیت برای ارزیابی و اطمینان از عملکرد صحیح نرم افزار
· نگهداری مستندات مربوط به عملکرد و ویژه گی های نرم افزار
· عیب یابی، اشکال زدایی و ارتقأ سیستم های موجود
· تشخیص و رفع مشکلات، عیب یابی و به روز رسانی سیستم های فعلی
· رعایت برنامه های پروژه و استاندارد های تکنالوژی
· تحلیل اطمینان از اپدیت بودن نرم افزار با ویژه گی های جدید
· حفظ مکاتبات و اسناد سازی
· ترتیب پلان های کاری و ارایۀ گزارش کاری به مدیر مستقیم و آمر ذیصلاح
· انجام سایر وظایف و مسئولیت های که بتأسی از طرزالعمل ها، دستورالعمل و هدایات مقام مافوق طبق لایحه و مقررات ارجاع میگردد

Job Requirements
درجه تحصیلی
· لیسانس در کمپیوتر ساینس، تکنالوژی معلوماتی اسناد تخصیصی مرتبط به وظیفه و به داشتن سند ماستری در بخش های فوق ترجیح داده میشود
تجربه
· سه (3) سال تجربۀ کاری مرتبط به وظیفه
مهارت ها و توانائی ها
· بلدیت به زبان های رسمی کشور و انگلیسی
· داشتن مهارت های مدیریتی
· فصاحت و بلاغت در گفتار
· آشنائی با برنامه های کمپیوتری Python, Django, FastAPI, .Net, React, Java+SpringBoot
· آشنائی به قوانین و مقررات بانکی
· توانائی در تحریر مکاتیب، پیشنهادات و گزارشات کاری
· فهم و درک مسئولیت کاری و تشخیص مشکلات در ساحۀ کاری
· حفظ محرمیت و ظیفوی
· آگاهی از وظایف و اهداف د افغانستان بانک
· توانایی طراحی و پیاده سازی برنامه های کاربردی که تعاملات کاربر را به خوبی مدیریت کند
· تسلط بر یکی یا چند زبان برنامه نویسی برای توسعۀ نرم افزار
· تسلط بر طراحی و مدیریت پایگاه های داده و استفاده از تکنالوژی های ORM برای ارتباط با پایگاه داده
· توانایی کار با فریم ورک های ویب برای توسعه و پیاده سازی برنامه های کاربردی ویب
· آشنایی با روش توسعه نرم افزار بر اساس تست، که شامل نوشتن تست های واحد قبل از کدنویسی است
· تسلط بر ابزار های مختلف مهندسی نرم افزار برای بهبود فرآیند های توسعه و تست
· قابلیت تهیه و نگهداری مستندات دقیق و کامل برای الزامات و مشخصات نرم افزار
صفات و ارزش های شخصی
صادق، دارای برخورد نیک، حامی احترام متقابل، مبتکر، با دسپلین، پابند به وظیفه و وقت، علاقمند به کار گروپی، همکار، مسئولیت پذیر، تصمیمگیر و مسلکی در بخش کاری

Submission Guidelines
نکات :

title: IT Manager
category: IT Billing, IT - Hardware, IT - Software, IT Support
description: Role Summary AurumPay is seeking an experienced and hands-on IT Manager to lead and manage our core technical infrastructure. As a licensed Electronic Money Institution (EMI), this role is critical in ensuring 99.99% uptime , security, and regulatory compliance of our digital wallet platform. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in Oracle Solaris environments and Sophos security infrastructure , and will act as a bridge between development, security, and operations teams to ensure systems are secure, scalable, and audit-ready. Duties and Responsibilities Key Responsibilities 1. Server Infrastructure (Oracle Solaris & Enterprise Systems) Lead the installation, configuration, and hardening of Oracle Solaris servers Configure and manage Automated Installer (AI) for network-based provisioning Administer Oracle Solaris Zones (containers) to isolate application environments Monitor system performance, including kernel tuning and resource management (ZFS, DTrace) Implement backup strategies, including ZFS snapshots and disaster recovery procedures 2. Network Security (Sophos Firewall & Perimeter Defense) Deploy, configure, and maintain Sophos Firewall (XG/XGS series) Implement firewall policies, VLAN segmentation, and access control lists (ACLs) Manage secure connectivity through Site-to-Site (IPsec/SSL) and SSL VPN Monitor and optimize threat protection tools, including MDR, IPS, Web Filtering, and Application Control 3. EMI & Fintech Compliance · Ensure all configurations align with Central Bank (DAB) regulations for EMI operations. · Maintain logs for all access to financial systems; prepare for internal and external audits. · Lead IT audit and change management processes – no unapproved changes to firewall, Solaris zones, or payment integrations. · Manage Business Continuity (BCP) and Disaster Recovery (DR) drills with measurable targets: RTO < 1 hour, RPO < 15 minutes for transaction systems. 4. IT Operations & Management Manage Windows/Linux servers where the application stack interfaces with Solaris. Oversee vendor management for internet links and hardware procurement. Lead and support the internal IT helpdesk team 5. Budget & Vendor Management (Critical addition) · Prepare and manage the annual IT budget, including hardware, software licenses (Oracle, Sophos), and support contracts. · Negotiate with vendors for internet links, data center services, and hardware procurement. Job Requirements Required Technical Skills & Competencies Expert-level experience with Oracle Solaris, including installation, Zones, ZFS, and networking Proficiency in Linux (RedHat/CentOS) and Windows Server environments Strong expertise in Sophos Firewall (XG/XGS), including policy configuration, NAT, VPN, and high availability Solid understanding of networking concepts such as VLANs, TCP/IP, and routing protocols (BGP/OSPF) Familiarity with fintech systems and standards such as ISO 8583, 3D Secure, PCI DSS, and high-availability architectures Experience with virtualization technologies such as Solaris Zones, VMware vSphere, or similar platforms Experience & Qualifications Minimum 5+ years of experience in IT infrastructure management At least 3 years of hands-on experience with Oracle Solaris and Sophos security solutions Prior experience in fintech, banking, or EMI environments is required Preferred Certifications: Oracle Certified Professional (OCP) – Solaris Sophos Certified Architect or Engineer CCNA, ITIL v4 Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field Personal Attributes High level of integrity and ability to handle sensitive financial systems Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work under pressure Detail-oriented with a strong focus on documentation and audit readiness Submission Guidelines How to Apply: Interested and qualified candidates are invited to submit their CV and cover letter (in PDF format) to jobs@aurumaf.com Please mention the position title “IT Manager” in the subject line of your email. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an interview.

title: Sourcing & Quotation Analyst
category: Procurement and logistics, Information Technology, Business Development, Sales, Supply Chain Management
description: Role Summary We are hiring a Sourcing & Quotation Analyst to join our remote team. This is a night shift role. Your shift starts at 5:00 PM Kabul time and runs for 8 hours, aligned with international business hours. Your day will revolve around three things: finding the right opportunities, sourcing the right items from the right vendors, and building clean quotations that win. You will spend most of your shift on calls and emails with international vendors, so strong English and confidence on the phone are non-negotiable. This role is for someone serious. Someone who wants to grow, works hard without being chased, and takes pride in getting the details right. If you are looking for a relaxed 9 to 5, this is not for you. If you want to build a real career and you are willing to put in the work, we want to talk to you. Duties and Responsibilities Opportunity Research Search online portals and platforms for new tenders, RFQs, and product opportunities. Read the requirements carefully and understand exactly what is being asked for. Shortlist the opportunities that fit and flag them to management. Item Sourcing Identify the correct manufacturers and authorized distributors for each item. Verify part numbers, specifications, and compliance requirements before reaching out. Find backup vendors for every key item so we are never stuck with a single source. Vendor Communication Call and email vendors during your shift to request quotes, confirm stock, lead times, and shipping terms. Ask sharp follow-up questions when a quote is incomplete or unclear. Follow up consistently until you have everything you need. No half-finished work. Quotation Preparation Organize vendor pricing in Excel or Google Sheets and compare properly. Build professional quotations using company templates. Triple check every number, SKU, quantity, and date before anything goes out. Coordination and Records Keep clean records of all vendor communication, quotes, and follow-ups. Update internal trackers and CRM tools daily. Work closely with management on final pricing and submission strategy. Never miss a deadline. Discipline and Work Ethic Log in on time. Every shift. Respond quickly on email, WhatsApp, and internal tools during your hours. Keep a professional tone in every vendor interaction. Own your work. No excuses, no shortcuts. Job Requirements Must-Have Fluent English. Spoken and written. You must be able to hold a clear, confident conversation on the phone with international vendors. This is not negotiable. Strong IT background. Comfortable with advanced Google search, Excel or Google Sheets (formulas, filters, sorting), email tools, cloud storage, and basic CRM systems. Sharp critical thinking. You can read a requirement, understand what is being asked, and spot gaps or risks in a vendor's quote. Night shift availability. Ready to work 5:00 PM to 1:00 AM Kabul time, Monday to Friday, without exception. Serious attention to detail. Numbers, SKUs, quantities, and dates must always be right. Discipline and work ethic. Self-driven, reliable, consistent. You do not need someone standing over you to get the work done. Confident on calls. Polite, professional, clear. No slang, no shortcuts. Hungry. You want to build something real and you are willing to put in the work to get there. Nice-to-Have Previous experience in procurement, sourcing, or supply chain. Exposure to international tenders or B2B quotation work. Experience with tender portals or sourcing platforms. Familiarity with Notion, ClickUp, Trello, or similar project management tools. Basic knowledge of IT hardware, industrial equipment, or logistics. Do Not Apply If You are looking for a 9 to 5. You cannot commit to the night shift. You need to be chased to finish work. Your English is weak and you plan to fake it on vendor calls. You cannot. Submission Guidelines Interested candidates should submit their updated CV along with a short introduction (2 to 3 paragraphs) explaining why they are a good fit for this role and confirming their availability for the night shift (5:00 PM to 1:00 AM Kabul time). Send applications to: procurement@vastglobelogistics.com Please write "Sourcing & Quotation Analyst Application" in the email subject line. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Applications without a proper introduction will not be considered.

description: About Speenkai Speenkai is Afghanistan's first and leading BPO (Business Process Outsourcing) service company, specializing in providing comprehensive administrative and technical support to Afghan-owned companies abroad. Our mission is to empower Afghan entrepreneurs and businesses operating internationally by offering high-quality, reliable support services from our base in Afghanistan. With a team of skilled professionals, we help Afghan companies streamline their operations, enhance efficiency, and achieve success. Job Summary Night-Shift Job Are you a motivated, detail-oriented professional fluent in English, and ready to grow in a dynamic environment? Speenkai, Afghanistan's first BPO service company, and Bay Master Appliance Services LLC are looking to hire experienced individuals to join our full-time night shift team as Dispatchers/Customer Service Representatives. In this role, you will take inbound calls, set appointments for customers, and dispatch technicians efficiently. The ideal candidate will take charge of daily operations, including planning, scheduling, and managing the tech force. PLEASE DO NOT APPLY IF YOU ARE NOT FLUENT IN ENGLISH to save yourself time. If you have strong communication skills, thrive under pressure, and are passionate about delivering exceptional support, we want to hear from you! Duties & Responsibilities Duties & Responsibilities 1. Receive incoming calls from clients and setup service appointments. 2. Receive incoming calls from technicians throughout the shift. 3. Keep track of daily schedule and job status throughout work schedule. 4. Respond to open email requests and inquiries via email 5. Ability to enter tickets into the CRM Job Requirements Job Requirements · Fluent in English and must possess excellent verbal and written skills. • Motivated self-starter • Office work experience • Ability to multitask in fast paced environment is crucial. • Capable of typing 45+ words per minute necessary • Customer Service skills and Conflict Resolution skills. • Telemarketing experience is a plus • Microsoft Office Products Submission Guideline This link will redirect you to an online assessment.
